reasoning: The informal statement asks to prove $x^2+1$ is irreducible in $\mathbb{F}_7$. The Lean statement quantifies over any field $F$ of cardinality 7 and claims $X^2+1$ is irreducible in $F[X]$. Since all finite fields of the same cardinality are isomorphic (any field of order 7 is isomorphic to $\mathbb{Z}/7\mathbb{Z} = \mathbb{F}_7$), and irreducibility of a polynomial transfers through ring isomorphisms, the two formulations are logically equivalent. The Lean signature correctly captures the claim without adding or dropping meaningful hypotheses.
